304	
305	## Skills — SKILL.md Format
306	
307	Source: [Agent Skills Specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) + [Codex skills docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/skills)
308	
309	### Minimal SKILL.md
310	
311	```markdown
312	---
313	name: my-skill
314	description: What this skill does and when to use it.
315	---
316	
317	Step-by-step instructions for the agent to follow.
318	```
319	
320	### Full SKILL.md with All Fields
321	
322	```markdown
323	---
324	name: my-skill
325	description: Detailed description of what this skill does. Include keywords for implicit matching. Max 1024 chars.
326	license: MIT
327	compatibility: Requires Node.js 20+ and git
328	metadata:
329	  author: my-org
330	  version: "1.0"
331	allowed-tools: Bash(git:*) Bash(npm:*) Read Write Edit
332	---
333	
334	## Overview
335	
336	Brief explanation of the workflow.
337	
338	## Steps
339	
340	1. First do X
341	2. Then do Y
342	3. Finally do Z
343	
344	## Examples
345	
346	### Input
347	User asks: "Deploy the project"
348	
349	### Expected behavior
350	1. Check build status
351	2. Run deployment command
352	3. Verify deployment succeeded
353	
354	## Edge Cases
355	
356	- If no project found, ask user to specify path
357	- If build fails, show error and suggest fixes
358	
359	## References
360	
361	See [detailed reference](references/REFERENCE.md) for API documentation.
362	```
363	
364	### Naming Rules (Strict)
365	
366	- 1-64 characters
367	- Lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only
368	- No leading/trailing hyphens
369	- No consecutive hyphens (`--`)
370	- **Must match parent directory name** (e.g., `my-skill/SKILL.md` must have `name: my-skill`)
371	
372	### Skill Directory Structure
373	
374	```
375	my-skill/
376	├── SKILL.md              # Required: metadata + instructions
377	├── scripts/              # Optional: executable code
378	│   └── deploy.sh
379	├── references/           # Optional: detailed docs loaded on demand
380	│   └── REFERENCE.md
381	├── assets/               # Optional: templates, schemas, images
382	│   └── template.json
383	└── agents/
384	    └── openai.yaml       # Optional: UI/policy metadata
385	```
386	
387	### Progressive Disclosure
388	
389	Codex loads skills in stages for context efficiency:
390	1. **Metadata** (~100 tokens): `name` + `description` loaded at startup for ALL skills
391	2. **Instructions** (< 5000 tokens recommended): Full SKILL.md body loaded when skill activates
392	3. **Resources** (on demand): Files in scripts/, references/, assets/ loaded only when needed
393	
394	**Best practice:** Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines. Move detailed reference material to separate files.
395	
396	---
397	
398	## Local Testing
399	
400	### Method 1: Use $plugin-creator (Recommended)
401	
402	Run `$plugin-creator` inside Codex. It scaffolds the `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` manifest and can generate a local marketplace entry for testing.
403	
404	### Method 2: Repo-Scoped Installation (Team Testing)
405	
406	1. Copy plugin to `$REPO_ROOT/plugins/my-plugin/`
407	2. Create `$REPO_ROOT/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`:
408	
409	```json
410	{
411	  "name": "local-repo",
412	  "plugins": [
413	    {
414	      "name": "my-plugin",
415	      "source": {
416	        "source": "local",
417	        "path": "./plugins/my-plugin"
418	      },
419	      "policy": {
420	        "installation": "AVAILABLE",
421	        "authentication": "ON_INSTALL"
422	      },
423	      "category": "Productivity"
424	    }
425	  ]
426	}
427	```
428	
429	3. Restart Codex
430	4. Browse with `/plugins` command or Codex app
431	
432	### Method 3: Personal Installation (Individual Testing)
433	
434	1. Copy plugin to `~/.codex/plugins/my-plugin/`
435	2. Create `~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` with same structure (change `name` to `"local-personal"`)
436	3. Restart Codex
437	
438	### marketplace.json Field Reference
439	
440	| Field | Values | Meaning |
441	|-------|--------|---------|
442	| `source.source` | `"local"` | Plugin loaded from filesystem |
443	| `source.path` | `"./plugins/name"` | Relative path from marketplace root. Must start with `./`, must stay within root |
444	| `policy.installation` | `"AVAILABLE"`, `"INSTALLED_BY_DEFAULT"`, `"NOT_AVAILABLE"` | Installation behavior |
445	| `policy.authentication` | `"ON_INSTALL"` | When auth happens |
446	| `category` | `"Productivity"`, `"Coding"`, `"Design"`, etc. | Plugin category |
447	
448	### Post-Installation Storage
449	
450	Codex stores installed plugins at:
451	```
452	~/.codex/plugins/cache/$MARKETPLACE_NAME/$PLUGIN_NAME/$VERSION/
453	```
454	For local plugins, `$VERSION` is `local`.
455	
456	### Testing Checklist
457	
458	1. Plugin appears in `/plugins` list
459	2. Skills trigger correctly (test both explicit `@skill-name` and implicit description matching)
460	3. MCP servers connect and tools are available (check with `/mcp`)
461	4. App integrations authenticate correctly
462	5. Hooks fire on expected tool use events
463	6. Icons and branding display correctly
464	
465	---
466	
467	## Publishing to the Official Directory
468	
469	### Current Status (as of 2026-03-28)
470	
471	**Self-serve publishing is NOT yet available.** The official documentation states:
472	
473	> "Adding plugins to the official Plugin Directory is coming soon."
474	> "Self-serve plugin publishing and management are coming soon."
475	
476	The official directory currently contains only OpenAI-curated plugins. A [community forum thread](https://community.openai.REDACTED) asking about third-party publishing received no official response.
477	
478	### Distribution Alternatives (Available Now)
479	
480	| Method | Scope | How |
481	|--------|-------|-----|
482	| **Repo marketplace** | Team/org | `$REPO_ROOT/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` pointing to local plugin dir |
483	| **Personal marketplace** | Individual | `~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` pointing to `~/.codex/plugins/` |
484	| **Git repository** | Public | Share repo URL, users clone into their plugins directory |
485	| **npm package** | Public | Publish MCP server as npm package, reference in .mcp.json |
486	
487	### What to Prepare for Official Directory Submission
488	
489	Based on the `interface` fields and patterns from official plugins, prepare:
490	
491	1. **Branding assets:**
492	   - `composerIcon` — Small SVG or PNG icon for the Codex composer
493	   - `logo` — Full logo PNG
494	   - `screenshots` — Array of screenshot PNGs showing the plugin in action
495	
496	2. **Legal:**
497	   - `privacyPolicyURL` — Published privacy policy
498	   - `termsOfServiceURL` — Published terms of service
499	   - `license` — Open source license (MIT recommended based on official plugins)
500	
501	3. **Metadata:**
502	   - Descriptive `keywords` array for discoverability
503	   - Clear `shortDescription` and `longDescription`
504	   - Relevant `category`
505	   - Accurate `capabilities` array
506	   - Helpful `defaultPrompt` (string or array of example prompts)
507	
508	4. **Quality:**
509	   - Working skills with clear descriptions for implicit matching
510	   - MCP servers accessible and responsive
511	   - README with usage instructions
512	
513	---
514	
515	## Asset Requirements
516	
517	Source: Official plugins repo (verified SVG + PNG usage)
518	
519	| Asset | Format | Purpose | Required |
520	|-------|--------|---------|----------|
521	| `composerIcon` | SVG or PNG | Small icon in Codex composer UI | Recommended |
522	| `logo` | PNG | Full logo for directory listing | Recommended |
523	| `screenshots` | PNG | Screenshots of plugin in action | Optional |
524	
525	The Figma plugin uses:
526	- `./assets/figma-small.svg` for `composerIcon`
527	- `./assets/figma.png` for `logo`
528	
529	No specific dimension requirements found in docs, but based on typical usage: icon ~24-48px, logo ~128-256px, screenshots ~1280x720px or similar.
530

532	
533	## Authentication Handling
534	
535	### MCP Server Auth
536	
537	For HTTP/streamable MCP servers requiring auth:
538	
539	```toml
540	# In config.toml (after install)
541	[mcp_servers.my-server]
542	url = "https://mcp.example.com"
543	bearer_token_env_var = "MY_API_TOKEN"
544	```
545	
546	Or with OAuth:
547	```bash
548	codex mcp login my-server
549	```
550	
551	OAuth config in `config.toml`:
552	```toml
553	mcp_oauth_callback_port = 5555
554	mcp_oauth_callback_url = "https://devbox.example.internal/callback"
555	```
556	
557	### App Connector Auth
558	
559	Apps use the ChatGPT connector system. Auth happens at install time (`policy.authentication: "ON_INSTALL"`). Users sign into the external service through OAuth flows managed by OpenAI.
560	
561	### Plugin-Level Auth Flow
562	
563	When a plugin is installed:
564	1. Skills activate immediately (no auth needed)
565	2. Apps may require sign-in to external services
566	3. MCP servers may need additional setup (API keys, OAuth)
567

633	## OpenBotCity Plugin — Recommended Structure
634	
635	Based on all findings, here is the recommended structure for an OpenBotCity Codex plugin:
636	
637	```
638	openbotcity/
639	├── .codex-plugin/
640	│   └── plugin.json
641	├── agents/
642	│   └── openai.yaml
643	├── skills/
644	│   ├── register-agent/
645	│   │   └── SKILL.md
646	│   ├── manage-agent/
647	│   │   └── SKILL.md
648	│   ├── explore-city/
649	│   │   └── SKILL.md
650	│   └── research-quests/
651	│       └── SKILL.md
652	├── .mcp.json
653	├── assets/
654	│   ├── openbotcity-small.svg
655	│   ├── openbotcity.png
656	│   └── screenshot-city.png
657	└── README.md
658	```
659	
660	### plugin.json for OpenBotCity
661	
662	```json
663	{
664	  "name": "openbotcity",

728	| 2026-03-26 | CLI 0.117.0 | Plugins became first-class: startup sync, `/plugins` browsing, install/remove with auth handling |
729	| 2026-03-25 | — | Plugin marketplace integration across app, CLI, IDE. Curated public directory launched |
730	| 2026-03-19 | CLI 0.116.0 | Plugin setup improvements: missing plugin prompts, suggestion allowlists, remote state sync |
731	
732	**Key takeaway:** The plugin system is brand new (days old as of this research). APIs and requirements may change rapidly.
733	
734	---
735	
736	## Open Questions
737	
738	1. **Official directory submission process** — Not yet defined. No timeline given. Monitor the [changelog](https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog) and [community forums](https://community.openai.com).
739	
740	2. **Exact .mcp.json schema for STDIO servers in plugins** — The HTTP format is verified from the official repo. STDIO format in .mcp.json is inferred from config.toml patterns but not explicitly shown in any official plugin example (all official plugins use HTTP MCP servers).
741	
742	3. **App connector IDs** — The `connector_` ID format in `.app.json` appears to be assigned by OpenAI. No documentation on how to register a new connector. This may only apply to first-party integrations.
743	
744	4. **Asset dimension requirements** — No explicit size requirements found. Following SVG for icons and PNG for logos based on official plugin patterns.
745	
746	5. **Review/approval criteria** — Unknown until self-serve publishing launches. Expect requirements around privacy policy, terms of service, working MCP endpoints, and quality skills.
747	
748	---
749	
750	## Sources
751	
752	### PRIMARY (HIGH confidence)
753	- [Build Plugins — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins/build) — Manifest schema, directory structure, installation
754	- [Plugins Overview — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins) — Concepts, components, usage
755	- [Skills — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/skills) — Skill format, discovery, openai.yaml
756	- [MCP — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp) — MCP config, transports, OAuth
757	- [Changelog — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog) — Release history
758	- [openai/plugins GitHub repo](https://github.com/openai/plugins) — Real plugin examples (Figma, Notion, Build Web Apps, Netlify)
759	- [Agent Skills Specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) — SKILL.md format spec
760	- [openai/skills GitHub repo](https://github.com/openai/skills) — Skill examples
761	
762	### SECONDARY (MEDIUM confidence)
763	- [SiliconANGLE coverage](https://siliconangle.com/2026/03/27/openai-introduces-plugins-codex-programming-assistant/) — Plugin marketplace launch context
764	- [The Decoder coverage](https://the-decoder.com/openais-codex-gets-a-plugin-marketplace-for-slack-notion-figma-and-more/) — Marketplace details
765	- [Community Forum — Publishing Question](https://community.openai.REDACTED) — No official answer yet
766	
767	### TERTIARY (LOW confidence)
768	- [awesome-codex-plugins](https://github.com/noahfraiture/awesome-codex-plugins) — Empty community list (no real plugins yet)
769	
770	---
771	
772	## Metadata
773	
774	**Confidence breakdown:**
775	- Directory structure: HIGH — verified against 4 official plugins in openai/plugins repo
776	- plugin.json schema: HIGH — verified against 4 real manifests
777	- .mcp.json format: HIGH for HTTP, MEDIUM for STDIO — HTTP verified from build-web-apps plugin
778	- .app.json format: HIGH — verified from figma plugin
779	- Skills format: HIGH — verified against agentskills.io spec + official docs
780	- hooks.json: MEDIUM — one example from figma plugin only
781	- Local testing: HIGH — documented in official build guide
782	- Publishing process: HIGH confidence that it does NOT exist yet
783	
784	**Research date:** 2026-03-28
785	**Valid until:** 2026-04-07 (system is brand new and changing rapidly — re-verify before implementation)
786

file.content:
# Research: Building and Publishing a Codex Plugin

**Researched:** 2026-03-28
**Domain:** OpenAI Codex Plugin System
**Confidence:** HIGH (official docs + official repo examples verified)

## Summary

OpenAI Codex plugins are installable bundles that package **skills** (reusable instruction workflows), **app integrations** (connections to external services), and **MCP server configurations** into a single distributable unit. The plugin system became first-class in Codex CLI 0.117.0 (2026-03-26) and is supported across the Codex app, CLI, and IDE extensions.

**The official Plugin Directory is curated by OpenAI only.** Self-serve publishing is announced as "coming soon" with no confirmed timeline. In the meantime, plugins can be distributed via local per-repo marketplaces (`marketplace.json`) and personal per-user marketplaces, or shared as Git repositories.

**Primary recommendation:** Build the plugin following the exact structure from `github.com/openai/plugins`, distribute initially via the repo marketplace pattern, and be ready to submit to the official directory when self-serve opens.

---

## Plugin Directory Structure (Verified)

Source: [Official build guide](https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins/build) + [openai/plugins repo](https://github.com/openai/plugins)

```
my-plugin/
├── .codex-plugin/
│   └── plugin.json          # REQUIRED — manifest
├── skills/                   # Optional — skill directories
│   ├── skill-one/
│   │   └── SKILL.md
│   └── skill-two/
│       ├── SKILL.md
│       ├── scripts/          # Optional executable code
│       ├── references/       # Optional docs
│       └── assets/           # Optional templates/resources
├── agents/                   # Optional — agent metadata
│   └── openai.yaml
├── .mcp.json                 # Optional — MCP server configs
├── .app.json                 # Optional — app/connector mappings
├── hooks.json                # Optional — lifecycle hooks
├── commands/                 # Optional — custom commands
├── assets/                   # Plugin-level visual assets
│   ├── icon.png              # Composer icon
│   ├── logo.png              # Full logo
│   └── screenshot-1.png      # Screenshots for directory
├── scripts/                  # Plugin-level scripts
└── README.md
```

**Critical rule:** Only `plugin.json` belongs inside `.codex-plugin/`. Everything else lives at the plugin root.

---

## plugin.json Manifest — Complete Schema

Source: [Official docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins/build) + verified against [Figma](https://github.com/openai/plugins/blob/main/plugins/figma/.codex-plugin/plugin.json), [Notion](https://github.com/openai/plugins/blob/main/plugins/notion/.codex-plugin/plugin.json), and [Build Web Apps](https://github.com/openai/plugins/blob/main/plugins/build-web-apps/.codex-plugin/plugin.json) plugins.

```jsonc
{
  // === IDENTIFICATION (required) ===
  "name": "my-plugin",              // Kebab-case, stable ID. Used as namespace.
  "version": "0.1.0",               // Semver
  "description": "What the plugin does in one sentence.",

  // === AUTHOR (optional but recommended) ===
  "author": {
    "name": "Your Team",
    "email": "team@example.com",     // Optional
    "url": "https://example.com"     // Optional
  },

  // === LINKS (optional) ===
  "homepage": "https://example.com",
  "repository": "https://github.com/org/repo",
  "license": "MIT",
  "keywords": ["keyword1", "keyword2"],

  // === COMPONENT POINTERS (at least one recommended) ===
  "skills": "./skills/",            // Path to skills directory
  "mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",      // Path to MCP config file
  "apps": "./.app.json",            // Path to app integrations file
  "hooks": "./hooks.json",          // Path to lifecycle hooks

  // === INTERFACE / PRESENTATION (for directory listing) ===
  "interface": {
    "displayName": "My Plugin",                    // Human-readable title
    "shortDescription": "Brief summary",           // One liner
    "longDescription": "Detailed description...",   // Multi-sentence
    "developerName": "Your Team",                  // Publisher name
    "category": "Productivity",                    // See categories below
    "capabilities": ["Read", "Write"],             // See capabilities below
    "websiteURL": "https://example.com",
    "privacyPolicyURL": "https://example.com/privacy",
    "termsOfServiceURL": "https://example.com/terms",
    "defaultPrompt": "Use My Plugin to do X",      // String OR array of strings
    "brandColor": "#10A37F",                       // Hex color
    "composerIcon": "./assets/icon.svg",           // Small icon (SVG or PNG)
    "logo": "./assets/logo.png",                   // Full logo (PNG)
    "screenshots": ["./assets/screenshot-1.png"]   // Array of paths
  }
}
```

### Known Categories (from official plugins)

| Category | Used By |
|----------|---------|
| `"Design"` | Figma |
| `"Productivity"` | Notion |
| `"Coding"` | Build Web Apps, Netlify |

### Known Capabilities

| Capability | Meaning |
|------------|---------|
| `"Read"` | Plugin reads data from external services |
| `"Write"` | Plugin writes/modifies external data |
| `"Interactive"` | Plugin requires user interaction (auth, prompts) |

### Naming Conventions

- **Plugin name:** Kebab-case only (e.g., `my-awesome-plugin`)
- **Skill names:** Kebab-case, must match parent directory name
- **Manifest path:** Always `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` (exactly this)
- **Asset paths:** Relative from plugin root, starting with `./`

---

## .mcp.json — MCP Server Configuration

Source: [build-web-apps/.mcp.json](https://github.com/openai/plugins/blob/main/plugins/build-web-apps/.mcp.json) (verified)

### HTTP/Streamable Transport (remote servers)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "my-service": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.example.com"
    }
  }
}
```

Real example from the build-web-apps plugin:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "stripe": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.stripe.com"
    },
    "vercel": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.vercel.com"
    },
    "supabase": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

### STDIO Transport (local process servers)

Based on Codex config.toml format (these fields map into .mcp.json):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "my-local-server": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "my-mcp-server-package"],
      "env": {
        "API_KEY": "${API_KEY}"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### For our OpenBotCity MCP server specifically

Since our MCP server is deployed at `mcp.openbotcity.com` (Cloudflare Worker with streamable HTTP):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "openbotcity": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

Or for local development via npm:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "openbotcity": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "openbotcity-mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Linking from plugin.json

In `plugin.json`, point to the file:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": "./.mcp.json"
}
```

---

## .app.json — App/Connector Configuration

Source: [figma/.app.json](https://github.com/openai/plugins/blob/main/plugins/figma/.app.json) (verified)

```json
{
  "apps": {
    "figma": {
      "id": "connector_68df038e0ba48191908c8434991bbac2"
    }
  }
}
```

Each entry maps an app name to a connector ID. These connector IDs are assigned by OpenAI/ChatGPT's connector system. If your plugin does not integrate with ChatGPT connectors (Gmail, Slack, etc.), you can omit `.app.json` entirely.

---

## hooks.json — Lifecycle Hooks

Source: [figma/hooks.json](https://github.com/openai/plugins/blob/main/plugins/figma/hooks.json) (verified)

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Write|Edit",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "./scripts/post_write_parity_check.sh"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Hooks fire after tool invocations. The `matcher` field uses pipe-delimited tool names. `PostToolUse` is the only confirmed hook event type from the official repo.

---

## agents/openai.yaml — Agent Metadata

Source: [figma/agents/openai.yaml](https://github.com/openai/plugins/blob/main/plugins/figma/agents/openai.yaml) (verified)

```yaml
interface:
  display_name: "My Plugin"
  short_description: "What this plugin does"
  icon_small: "./assets/icon-small.svg"
  icon_large: "./assets/icon.png"
  default_prompt: "Use My Plugin to accomplish X."
```

This file controls how the plugin appears in Codex's agent surface. Fields mirror the `interface` section in `plugin.json` but use snake_case. Additional fields supported (from skills docs):

```yaml
interface:
  display_name: "My Plugin"
  short_description: "Brief description"
  icon_small: "./assets/small-logo.svg"
  icon_large: "./assets/large-logo.png"
  brand_color: "#3B82F6"
  default_prompt: "Optional surrounding prompt"

policy:
  allow_implicit_invocation: false  # Default: true

dependencies:
  tools:
    - type: "mcp"
      value: "my-mcp-server"
      description: "Description of MCP server"
      transport: "streamable_http"
      url: "https://mcp.example.com"
```

---

## Skills — SKILL.md Format

Source: [Agent Skills Specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) + [Codex skills docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/skills)

### Minimal SKILL.md

```markdown
---
name: my-skill
description: What this skill does and when to use it.
---

Step-by-step instructions for the agent to follow.
```

### Full SKILL.md with All Fields

```markdown
---
name: my-skill
description: Detailed description of what this skill does. Include keywords for implicit matching. Max 1024 chars.
license: MIT
compatibility: Requires Node.js 20+ and git
metadata:
  author: my-org
  version: "1.0"
allowed-tools: Bash(git:*) Bash(npm:*) Read Write Edit
---

## Overview

Brief explanation of the workflow.

## Steps

1. First do X
2. Then do Y
3. Finally do Z

## Examples

### Input
User asks: "Deploy the project"

### Expected behavior
1. Check build status
2. Run deployment command
3. Verify deployment succeeded

## Edge Cases

- If no project found, ask user to specify path
- If build fails, show error and suggest fixes

## References

See [detailed reference](references/REFERENCE.md) for API documentation.
```

### Naming Rules (Strict)

- 1-64 characters
- Lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only
- No leading/trailing hyphens
- No consecutive hyphens (`--`)
- **Must match parent directory name** (e.g., `my-skill/SKILL.md` must have `name: my-skill`)

### Skill Directory Structure

```
my-skill/
├── SKILL.md              # Required: metadata + instructions
├── scripts/              # Optional: executable code
│   └── deploy.sh
├── references/           # Optional: detailed docs loaded on demand
│   └── REFERENCE.md
├── assets/               # Optional: templates, schemas, images
│   └── template.json
└── agents/
    └── openai.yaml       # Optional: UI/policy metadata
```

### Progressive Disclosure

Codex loads skills in stages for context efficiency:
1. **Metadata** (~100 tokens): `name` + `description` loaded at startup for ALL skills
2. **Instructions** (< 5000 tokens recommended): Full SKILL.md body loaded when skill activates
3. **Resources** (on demand): Files in scripts/, references/, assets/ loaded only when needed

**Best practice:** Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines. Move detailed reference material to separate files.

---

## Local Testing

### Method 1: Use $plugin-creator (Recommended)

Run `$plugin-creator` inside Codex. It scaffolds the `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` manifest and can generate a local marketplace entry for testing.

### Method 2: Repo-Scoped Installation (Team Testing)

1. Copy plugin to `$REPO_ROOT/plugins/my-plugin/`
2. Create `$REPO_ROOT/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "local-repo",
  "plugins": [
    {
      "name": "my-plugin",
      "source": {
        "source": "local",
        "path": "./plugins/my-plugin"
      },
      "policy": {
        "installation": "AVAILABLE",
        "authentication": "ON_INSTALL"
      },
      "category": "Productivity"
    }
  ]
}
```

3. Restart Codex
4. Browse with `/plugins` command or Codex app

### Method 3: Personal Installation (Individual Testing)

1. Copy plugin to `~/.codex/plugins/my-plugin/`
2. Create `~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` with same structure (change `name` to `"local-personal"`)
3. Restart Codex

### marketplace.json Field Reference

| Field | Values | Meaning |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `source.source` | `"local"` | Plugin loaded from filesystem |
| `source.path` | `"./plugins/name"` | Relative path from marketplace root. Must start with `./`, must stay within root |
| `policy.installation` | `"AVAILABLE"`, `"INSTALLED_BY_DEFAULT"`, `"NOT_AVAILABLE"` | Installation behavior |
| `policy.authentication` | `"ON_INSTALL"` | When auth happens |
| `category` | `"Productivity"`, `"Coding"`, `"Design"`, etc. | Plugin category |

### Post-Installation Storage

Codex stores installed plugins at:
```
~/.codex/plugins/cache/$MARKETPLACE_NAME/$PLUGIN_NAME/$VERSION/
```
For local plugins, `$VERSION` is `local`.

### Testing Checklist

1. Plugin appears in `/plugins` list
2. Skills trigger correctly (test both explicit `@skill-name` and implicit description matching)
3. MCP servers connect and tools are available (check with `/mcp`)
4. App integrations authenticate correctly
5. Hooks fire on expected tool use events
6. Icons and branding display correctly

---

## Publishing to the Official Directory

### Current Status (as of 2026-03-28)

**Self-serve publishing is NOT yet available.** The official documentation states:

> "Adding plugins to the official Plugin Directory is coming soon."
> "Self-serve plugin publishing and management are coming soon."

The official directory currently contains only OpenAI-curated plugins. A [community forum thread](https://community.openai.REDACTED) asking about third-party publishing received no official response.

### Distribution Alternatives (Available Now)

| Method | Scope | How |
|--------|-------|-----|
| **Repo marketplace** | Team/org | `$REPO_ROOT/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` pointing to local plugin dir |
| **Personal marketplace** | Individual | `~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` pointing to `~/.codex/plugins/` |
| **Git repository** | Public | Share repo URL, users clone into their plugins directory |
| **npm package** | Public | Publish MCP server as npm package, reference in .mcp.json |

### What to Prepare for Official Directory Submission

Based on the `interface` fields and patterns from official plugins, prepare:

1. **Branding assets:**
   - `composerIcon` — Small SVG or PNG icon for the Codex composer
   - `logo` — Full logo PNG
   - `screenshots` — Array of screenshot PNGs showing the plugin in action

2. **Legal:**
   - `privacyPolicyURL` — Published privacy policy
   - `termsOfServiceURL` — Published terms of service
   - `license` — Open source license (MIT recommended based on official plugins)

3. **Metadata:**
   - Descriptive `keywords` array for discoverability
   - Clear `shortDescription` and `longDescription`
   - Relevant `category`
   - Accurate `capabilities` array
   - Helpful `defaultPrompt` (string or array of example prompts)

4. **Quality:**
   - Working skills with clear descriptions for implicit matching
   - MCP servers accessible and responsive
   - README with usage instructions

---

## Asset Requirements

Source: Official plugins repo (verified SVG + PNG usage)

| Asset | Format | Purpose | Required |
|-------|--------|---------|----------|
| `composerIcon` | SVG or PNG | Small icon in Codex composer UI | Recommended |
| `logo` | PNG | Full logo for directory listing | Recommended |
| `screenshots` | PNG | Screenshots of plugin in action | Optional |

The Figma plugin uses:
- `./assets/figma-small.svg` for `composerIcon`
- `./assets/figma.png` for `logo`

No specific dimension requirements found in docs, but based on typical usage: icon ~24-48px, logo ~128-256px, screenshots ~1280x720px or similar.

---

## Authentication Handling

### MCP Server Auth

For HTTP/streamable MCP servers requiring auth:

```toml
# In config.toml (after install)
[mcp_servers.my-server]
url = "https://mcp.example.com"
bearer_token_env_var = "MY_API_TOKEN"
```

Or with OAuth:
```bash
codex mcp login my-server
```

OAuth config in `config.toml`:
```toml
mcp_oauth_callback_port = 5555
mcp_oauth_callback_url = "https://devbox.example.internal/callback"
```

### App Connector Auth

Apps use the ChatGPT connector system. Auth happens at install time (`policy.authentication: "ON_INSTALL"`). Users sign into the external service through OAuth flows managed by OpenAI.

### Plugin-Level Auth Flow

When a plugin is installed:
1. Skills activate immediately (no auth needed)
2. Apps may require sign-in to external services
3. MCP servers may need additional setup (API keys, OAuth)

---

## Real-World Examples from Official Repo

### Example 1: Figma Plugin (Design, Apps + Hooks)

```
figma/
├── .codex-plugin/plugin.json    # category: "Design"
├── agents/openai.yaml
├── skills/
│   ├── implement-design/        # Figma frame to code
│   ├── code-connect-components/ # Component mapping
│   └── create-design-system-rules/
├── .app.json                    # Figma connector
├── hooks.json                   # PostToolUse parity check
├── scripts/
│   └── post_write_figma_parity_check.sh
└── assets/
```

### Example 2: Build Web Apps (Coding, MCP Servers)

```
build-web-apps/
├── .codex-plugin/plugin.json    # category: "Coding"
├── agents/openai.yaml
├── skills/
│   ├── deploy-to-vercel/
│   ├── react-best-practices/
│   ├── shadcn-best-practices/
│   ├── stripe-best-practices/
│   ├── supabase-best-practices/
│   └── web-design-guidelines/
├── .mcp.json                    # Stripe, Vercel, Supabase MCP
└── assets/
```

### Example 3: Notion (Productivity, Apps Only)

```
notion/
├── .codex-plugin/plugin.json    # category: "Productivity"
├── skills/
│   ├── planning/
│   ├── research/
│   ├── meetings/
│   └── knowledge-capture/
├── .app.json                    # Notion connector
└── assets/
```

### Example 4: Netlify (Coding, Apps Only)

```
netlify/
├── .codex-plugin/plugin.json    # category: "Coding"
├── skills/
│   └── netlify-deploy/
├── .app.json
└── assets/
```

---

## OpenBotCity Plugin — Recommended Structure

Based on all findings, here is the recommended structure for an OpenBotCity Codex plugin:

```
openbotcity/
├── .codex-plugin/
│   └── plugin.json
├── agents/
│   └── openai.yaml
├── skills/
│   ├── register-agent/
│   │   └── SKILL.md
│   ├── manage-agent/
│   │   └── SKILL.md
│   ├── explore-city/
│   │   └── SKILL.md
│   └── research-quests/
│       └── SKILL.md
├── .mcp.json
├── assets/
│   ├── openbotcity-small.svg
│   ├── openbotcity.png
│   └── screenshot-city.png
└── README.md
```

### plugin.json for OpenBotCity

```json
{
  "name": "openbotcity",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Register and manage AI agents in OpenBotCity, a futuristic Matrix-universe city where AI agents live, socialize, and create.",
  "author": {
    "name": "OpenBotCity",
    "url": "https://openbotcity.com"
  },
  "homepage": "https://openbotcity.com",
  "repository": "https://github.com/openclawcity/mcp",
  "license": "MIT",
  "keywords": [
    "openbotcity",
    "openclawcity",
    "ai-agents",
    "virtual-city",
    "mcp",
    "agent-simulation"
  ],
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",
  "interface": {
    "displayName": "OpenBotCity",
    "shortDescription": "Register and manage AI agents in a virtual city",
    "longDescription": "Use OpenBotCity to register AI agents, manage their presence in the city, explore buildings and zones, participate in research quests, and interact with other agents in a living virtual environment.",
    "developerName": "OpenBotCity",
    "category": "Coding",
    "capabilities": ["Interactive", "Read", "Write"],
    "websiteURL": "https://openbotcity.com",
    "privacyPolicyURL": "https://openbotcity.com/privacy",
    "termsOfServiceURL": "https://openbotcity.com/terms",
    "defaultPrompt": [
      "Register a new agent in OpenBotCity",
      "Check my agent's status in the city",
      "Explore what's happening in OpenBotCity"
    ],
    "brandColor": "#10A37F",
    "composerIcon": "./assets/openbotcity-small.svg",
    "logo": "./assets/openbotcity.png",
    "screenshots": []
  }
}
```

### .mcp.json for OpenBotCity

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "openbotcity": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Changelog — Plugin-Related Updates

Source: [Codex Changelog](https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog)

| Date | Version | Change |
|------|---------|--------|
| 2026-03-26 | CLI 0.117.0 | Plugins became first-class: startup sync, `/plugins` browsing, install/remove with auth handling |
| 2026-03-25 | — | Plugin marketplace integration across app, CLI, IDE. Curated public directory launched |
| 2026-03-19 | CLI 0.116.0 | Plugin setup improvements: missing plugin prompts, suggestion allowlists, remote state sync |

**Key takeaway:** The plugin system is brand new (days old as of this research). APIs and requirements may change rapidly.

---

## Open Questions

1. **Official directory submission process** — Not yet defined. No timeline given. Monitor the [changelog](https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog) and [community forums](https://community.openai.com).

2. **Exact .mcp.json schema for STDIO servers in plugins** — The HTTP format is verified from the official repo. STDIO format in .mcp.json is inferred from config.toml patterns but not explicitly shown in any official plugin example (all official plugins use HTTP MCP servers).

3. **App connector IDs** — The `connector_` ID format in `.app.json` appears to be assigned by OpenAI. No documentation on how to register a new connector. This may only apply to first-party integrations.

4. **Asset dimension requirements** — No explicit size requirements found. Following SVG for icons and PNG for logos based on official plugin patterns.

5. **Review/approval criteria** — Unknown until self-serve publishing launches. Expect requirements around privacy policy, terms of service, working MCP endpoints, and quality skills.

---

## Sources

### PRIMARY (HIGH confidence)
- [Build Plugins — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins/build) — Manifest schema, directory structure, installation
- [Plugins Overview — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins) — Concepts, components, usage
- [Skills — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/skills) — Skill format, discovery, openai.yaml
- [MCP — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp) — MCP config, transports, OAuth
- [Changelog — Official Docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog) — Release history
- [openai/plugins GitHub repo](https://github.com/openai/plugins) — Real plugin examples (Figma, Notion, Build Web Apps, Netlify)
- [Agent Skills Specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) — SKILL.md format spec
- [openai/skills GitHub repo](https://github.com/openai/skills) — Skill examples

### SECONDARY (MEDIUM confidence)
- [SiliconANGLE coverage](https://siliconangle.com/2026/03/27/openai-introduces-plugins-codex-programming-assistant/) — Plugin marketplace launch context
- [The Decoder coverage](https://the-decoder.com/openais-codex-gets-a-plugin-marketplace-for-slack-notion-figma-and-more/) — Marketplace details
- [Community Forum — Publishing Question](https://community.openai.REDACTED) — No official answer yet

### TERTIARY (LOW confidence)
- [awesome-codex-plugins](https://github.com/noahfraiture/awesome-codex-plugins) — Empty community list (no real plugins yet)

---

## Metadata

**Confidence breakdown:**
- Directory structure: HIGH — verified against 4 official plugins in openai/plugins repo
- plugin.json schema: HIGH — verified against 4 real manifests
- .mcp.json format: HIGH for HTTP, MEDIUM for STDIO — HTTP verified from build-web-apps plugin
- .app.json format: HIGH — verified from figma plugin
- Skills format: HIGH — verified against agentskills.io spec + official docs
- hooks.json: MEDIUM — one example from figma plugin only
- Local testing: HIGH — documented in official build guide
- Publishing process: HIGH confidence that it does NOT exist yet

**Research date:** 2026-03-28
**Valid until:** 2026-04-07 (system is brand new and changing rapidly — re-verify before implementation)

1	# ChatGPT App Directory: How to Get Listed
2	
3	**Researched:** 2026-03-28
4	**Domain:** OpenAI ChatGPT App Directory / MCP Server Listing
5	**Confidence:** HIGH (based on official OpenAI developer documentation)
6	**Our MCP Server:** https://mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp
7	
8	## Executive Summary
9	
10	OpenAI launched the **ChatGPT App Directory** in December 2025, replacing the old GPT Store with a full third-party app integration platform built on the **Model Context Protocol (MCP)**. This is exactly the marketplace we want to be listed in. The directory is live, submissions are open, and OpenAI is actively reviewing and approving third-party apps.
11	
12	**The path is clear:** Build an Apps SDK-compliant MCP server, complete organization verification on the OpenAI Platform Dashboard, submit through their dashboard with required metadata, pass their review, and get published. Our existing MCP server at `mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp` is already the right foundation -- we need to ensure it meets their specific requirements and then submit.
13	
14	**Primary recommendation:** Complete OpenAI organization verification immediately, audit our MCP server against their requirements (tool annotations, CORS, error handling), prepare listing assets (icon, screenshots, descriptions), and submit.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 1. Is There a Public Directory/Marketplace?
19	
20	**YES.** (HIGH confidence)
21	
22	The **ChatGPT App Directory** is live at [chatgpt.com/apps](https://chatgpt.com/apps). It is:
23	
24	- Accessible from the ChatGPT tools menu and directly via URL
25	- Browsable by users (featured apps, search by name)
26	- The successor to the GPT Store, but for real integrations (not just prompt wrappers)
27	- Built entirely on MCP -- every app in the directory is an MCP server
28	
29	Early apps include Spotify, Adobe Acrobat, Canva, Booking.com, MyFitnessPal, and AllTrails.
30	
31	**Source:** [OpenAI - Introducing Apps in ChatGPT](https://openai.com/index/introducing-apps-in-chatgpt/), [OpenAI - Developers Can Now Submit Apps](https://openai.com/index/developers-can-now-submit-apps-to-chatgpt/)
32	
33	---
34	
35	## 2. How to Submit/Register an MCP Server
36	
37	### Step-by-Step Submission Process
38	
39	1. **Complete Organization Verification**
40	   - Go to the [OpenAI Platform Dashboard](https://platform.openai.com)
41	   - Verify individual identity (personal name) OR business identity
42	   - You must have the **Owner role** in your organization
43	   - This is a prerequisite -- cannot submit without it
44	
45	2. **Ensure MCP Server Compliance**
46	   - Server must be publicly accessible via HTTPS
47	   - Must handle `POST`, `GET`, `DELETE` requests to `/mcp` path
48	   - Must support CORS for cross-origin requests
49	   - Must implement JSON-RPC 2.0 message protocol
50	   - Must define proper tool annotations (see Section 4)
51	
52	3. **Access the Submission Dashboard**
53	   - Navigate to the app management section in the OpenAI Platform Dashboard
54	   - Input MCP server URL and OAuth credentials (if applicable)
55	
56	4. **Fill Out Required Fields** (see Section 4 for full list)
57	
58	5. **Submit for Review**
59	   - Click submit
60	   - Receive confirmation email with a Case ID
61	   - Monitor status in the Dashboard
62	
63	6. **Wait for Review**
64	   - OpenAI performs automated scans and/or manual reviews
65	   - Timeline varies (no SLA published, no expedited option)
66	   - Approval notification comes via email
67	
68	7. **Publish**
69	   - Approved apps transition to publication via the dashboard
70	   - OpenAI automatically creates a Codex plugin as well
71	
72	**Source:** [Submit and Maintain Your App](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission)
73	
74	---
75	
76	## 3. Review / Approval Process
77	
78	**YES, there is a review process.** (HIGH confidence)
79	
80	OpenAI reviews every submission before it goes live. Reviews evaluate:
81	
82	| Review Area | What They Check |
83	|-------------|----------------|
84	| **MCP Connectivity** | Server reachable, tools respond correctly using provided credentials |
85	| **Test Cases** | Your submitted test prompts produce expected responses |
86	| **Data Handling** | Actual behavior matches stated privacy practices |
87	| **Tool Annotations** | `readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `openWorldHint` are accurate |
88	| **Guidelines Compliance** | No prohibited content, no ads, proper naming |
89	| **Auth (if applicable)** | Test credentials work, 2FA is not blocking review |
90	
91	### Common Rejection Reasons
92	
93	- **Incorrect tool annotations** -- "a common cause of rejection" per OpenAI docs
94	- Connection/stability issues with MCP server
95	- Test case failures (expected output doesn't match)
96	- Undisclosed data collection
97	- Keyword stuffing in descriptions
98	- Single-word dictionary terms as app names
99	- Missing privacy policy
100	- Apps that serve advertisements
101	
102	### Post-Approval Maintenance
103	
104	- Apps can be removed if "inactive, unstable, or non-compliant"
105	- To update: create a new draft version (not a new app), add release notes, resubmit
106	- Only one version can be under review at a time
107	
108	**Source:** [App Submission Guidelines](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/app-submission-guidelines), [Submit and Maintain Your App](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission)
109	
110	---
111	
112	## 4. Requirements for Listing
113	
114	### Required Submission Assets
115	
116	| Asset | Specification | Notes |
117	|-------|--------------|-------|
118	| **App Icon** | 64 x 64 px, under 5 KB | Small -- needs to be clean and recognizable |
119	| **App Name** | Max 30 characters | Avoid single-word dictionary terms. Name carries most weight in search indexation |
120	| **Short Description** | Brief summary | Clear, accurate, no keyword stuffing |
121	| **Long Description** | Detailed explanation | Explain what the app does and how |
122	| **Developer Name** | Organization name | Must match verified identity |
123	| **Privacy Policy URL** | Published policy | Must explain data categories, use purposes, recipient categories, user controls |
124	| **Terms & Conditions URL** | Published terms | Required |
125	| **Screenshots** | Must match actual functionality | Dimension requirements exist (not fully specified in docs) |
126	| **Verified Website** | Your domain | Must be verified |
127	| **MCP Server URL** | HTTPS endpoint | e.g., `https://mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp` |
128	| **Test Prompts & Responses** | Demo scenarios | Show expected behavior -- OpenAI tests these |
129	| **Localization Info** | Languages supported | Optional but recommended |
130	| **OAuth Credentials** | If auth required | Must include test account credentials for review |
131	
132	### Tool Definition Requirements
133	
134	Every tool exposed by your MCP server must have:
135	
136	- **Human-readable name** -- specific, action-oriented (e.g., `get_agent_status`, `search_agents`)
137	- **Clear description** -- explains purpose accurately
138	- **Proper annotations** (CRITICAL for approval):
139	  - `readOnlyHint`: true for data retrieval only
140	  - `destructiveHint`: true for write/delete operations
141	  - `openWorldHint`: true for external system interactions
142	- **Minimal input schema** -- request only what's necessary, no "just in case" fields
143	- **Unique names** within the app
144	
145	### Technical Requirements
146	
147	- HTTPS endpoint (mandatory)
148	- CORS support for cross-origin requests
149	- JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol
150	- Proper error handling (no crashes, graceful degradation)
151	- Low latency responses
152	- Unique `_meta.ui.domain` per app (if using UI components)
153	- Content Security Policy configuration (if using UI)
154	
155	**Source:** [App Submission Guidelines](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/app-submission-guidelines), [Build Your MCP Server](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/build/mcp-server), [ChatGPT App Directory Optimization](https://phiture.com/asostack/chat-gpt-app-directory/)
156	
157	---
158	
159	## 5. Submission API/Form
160	
161	**Dashboard-based submission** (no public API). (HIGH confidence)
162	
163	- Submissions go through the **OpenAI Platform Dashboard** (platform.openai.com)
164	- There is a dedicated app management section
165	- Fill in the form, upload assets, submit
166	- Track review status in the same dashboard
167	- Email notifications for status changes
168	
169	There is **no public API** for programmatic submission. It is a manual dashboard process.
170	
171	**Note:** "Self-serve plugin publishing is coming soon" according to OpenAI docs, suggesting the process may become more automated in the future.
172	
173	**Source:** [Submit and Maintain Your App](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission)
174	
175	---
176	
177	## 6. Featured/Curated Connectors
178	
179	**YES, there is a featured/curated tier.** (HIGH confidence)
180	
181	OpenAI distinguishes between basic publication and enhanced distribution:
182	
183	| Tier | What It Means |
184	|------|--------------|
185	| **Published** | App appears in directory, searchable by name |
186	| **Featured/Enhanced Distribution** | Directory placement, proactive suggestions by ChatGPT |
187	
188	### How to Get Featured
189	
190	Apps demonstrating **"strong real-world utility and high user satisfaction"** may receive:
191	- Prominent directory placement
192	- Proactive recommendations (ChatGPT suggests the app during relevant conversations)
193	
194	This is separate from basic publication approval -- it is earned through usage and quality, not applied for directly.
195	
196	### Discovery Mechanics (Current State)
197	
198	- **Name-based search** is the primary discovery method
199	- Name carries the **most weight** in search indexation
200	- Long-tail keyword matching is limited currently
201	- Individual keywords perform better than combined phrases
202	- No category browsing system documented yet (browsing appears to be search + featured)
203	
204	**Source:** [App Submission Guidelines](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/app-submission-guidelines), [ChatGPT App Directory Optimization](https://phiture.com/asostack/chat-gpt-app-directory/)
205	
206	---
207	
208	## 7. The ChatGPT "Apps" Ecosystem (Full Picture)
209	
210	### Architecture
211	
212	```
213	ChatGPT App = MCP Server + (Optional) Web UI Component
214	                |
215	                v
216	         Apps SDK (OpenAI's framework)
217	                |
218	                v
219	         App Directory (the marketplace)
220	                |
221	                v
222	         Also creates Codex Plugin automatically
223	```
224	
225	### Key Terminology
226	
227	| Term | Meaning |
228	|------|---------|
229	| **App** | The new name (replaced "Connector") -- an MCP server integrated with ChatGPT |
230	| **Connector** | Legacy term, being phased out in favor of "App" |
231	| **Apps SDK** | OpenAI's framework for building ChatGPT apps (wraps MCP) |
232	| **App Directory** | The marketplace at chatgpt.com/apps |
233	| **Developer Mode** | Testing mode in ChatGPT settings for unreviewed apps |
234	| **Codex Plugin** | Auto-created when app is published (for OpenAI's Codex product) |
235	
236	### Optional UI Component
237	
238	Apps can optionally include a web UI component that renders inside ChatGPT:
239	- Built as HTML/CSS/JS (or React, Vue, etc.)
240	- Rendered in an iframe
241	- Communicates via JSON-RPC over postMessage
242	- MIME type: `text/html;profile=mcp-app`
243	- Not required -- tools-only apps work fine
244	
245	### Commerce Restrictions
246	
247	If planning any transactional features:
248	- **Physical goods only** currently (no digital products, subscriptions, tokens, credits)
249	- External checkout required (redirect to your domain)
250	- No embedded third-party checkout
251	- No advertisements allowed
252	
253	### Developer Mode (For Testing Before Submission)
254	
255	Before submitting, you can test your MCP server in ChatGPT:
256	1. Settings > Apps & Connectors > Advanced settings > Enable Developer Mode
257	2. Settings > Connectors > Create
258	3. Enter HTTPS URL, name, description
259	4. Test in new chats by adding the connector from the + menu
260	
261	---
262	
263	## 8. Action Plan for OpenBotCity MCP Server
264	
265	### Immediate Steps
266	
267	1. **Verify Organization on OpenAI Platform**
268	   - Go to platform.openai.com
269	   - Complete business identity verification
270	   - Ensure you have Owner role
271	
272	2. **Audit MCP Server Compliance**
273	   - Verify CORS headers on `mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp`
274	   - Ensure JSON-RPC 2.0 compliance
275	   - Add tool annotations (`readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `openWorldHint`) to ALL tools
276	   - Test error handling (graceful failures, no crashes)
277	   - Verify low-latency responses
278	
279	3. **Prepare Listing Assets**
280	   - Design 64x64 px icon (under 5 KB)
281	   - Write app name (max 30 chars) -- e.g., "OpenBotCity" or "OpenBotCity AI City"
282	   - Write short + long descriptions (clear, no keyword stuffing)
283	   - Prepare screenshots showing actual tool functionality
284	   - Publish privacy policy and terms at openbotcity.com URLs
285	   - Verify openbotcity.com domain
286	
287	4. **Write Test Prompts**
288	   - Create 3-5 test prompts that demonstrate core functionality
289	   - Document expected responses for each
290	   - These will be tested during review
291	
292	5. **Test in Developer Mode**
293	   - Enable Developer Mode in ChatGPT
294	   - Add as connector, test thoroughly
295	   - Fix any issues found
296	
297	6. **Submit**
298	   - Fill out all fields in the OpenAI Platform Dashboard
299	   - Submit and track via Case ID
300	
301	### Naming Strategy
302	
303	Since name carries the most weight in search:
304	- "OpenBotCity" is clean and unique (good)
305	- Consider if adding a descriptor helps: "OpenBotCity - AI Agent City"
306	- Avoid generic terms that might get rejected
307	
308	---
309	
310	## Prohibited Content Checklist
311	
312	Verify our MCP server does NOT involve:
313	
314	- [x] No adult content
315	- [x] No gambling
316	- [x] No illegal goods
317	- [x] No prescription medications
318	- [x] No surveillance tools
319	- [x] No advertisements served through the app
320	- [x] No digital product sales (currently restricted)
321	
322	---
323	
324	## Open Questions
325	
326	1. **Screenshot dimensions** -- exact pixel requirements for screenshots not fully documented. Prepare multiple sizes (1280x800, 1920x1080) to be safe.
327	
328	2. **Review timeline** -- OpenAI explicitly says "timelines may vary" with no SLA. Early reports suggest days to weeks. Being in beta means this could fluctuate.
329	
330	3. **Category system** -- no formal category taxonomy documented yet. The directory appears to rely on search + featured placement rather than browsable categories.
331	
332	4. **Proactive suggestion criteria** -- what exactly triggers ChatGPT to proactively suggest your app is not documented beyond "strong real-world utility and high user satisfaction."
333	
334	5. **Codex Plugin auto-creation** -- when your app is published, OpenAI also creates a Codex plugin. Unclear if this requires any additional configuration from us.
335	
336	---
337	
338	## Sources
339	
340	### Primary (HIGH confidence)
341	- [OpenAI Apps SDK Quickstart](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/quickstart)
342	- [Build Your MCP Server](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/build/mcp-server)
343	- [Connect from ChatGPT](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/connect-chatgpt)
344	- [Submit and Maintain Your App](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission)
345	- [App Submission Guidelines](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/app-submission-guidelines)
346	- [OpenAI - Developers Can Now Submit Apps](https://openai.com/index/developers-can-now-submit-apps-to-chatgpt/)
347	- [OpenAI - Introducing Apps in ChatGPT](https://openai.com/index/introducing-apps-in-chatgpt/)
348	
349	### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
350	- [ChatGPT App Directory Optimization (Phiture)](https://phiture.com/asostack/chat-gpt-app-directory/)
351	- [VentureBeat - OpenAI App Submissions](https://venturebeat.com/technology/openai-now-accepting-chatgpt-app-submissions-from-third-party-devs-launches)
352	- [OpenAI Community Forum - App Store Submissions](https://community.openai.com/t/chatgpt-app-store-is-open-for-submissions/1369611)
353	
354	### Also Relevant
355	- [MCP Registry](https://modelcontextprotocol.info/tools/registry/) -- separate from ChatGPT, this is the community MCP registry (Anthropic, GitHub, Microsoft backed). Consider listing there too.
356	- [MCP Market](https://mcpmarket.com/) -- third-party MCP server discovery platform
357

# ChatGPT App Directory: How to Get Listed

**Researched:** 2026-03-28
**Domain:** OpenAI ChatGPT App Directory / MCP Server Listing
**Confidence:** HIGH (based on official OpenAI developer documentation)
**Our MCP Server:** https://mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp

## Executive Summary

OpenAI launched the **ChatGPT App Directory** in December 2025, replacing the old GPT Store with a full third-party app integration platform built on the **Model Context Protocol (MCP)**. This is exactly the marketplace we want to be listed in. The directory is live, submissions are open, and OpenAI is actively reviewing and approving third-party apps.

**The path is clear:** Build an Apps SDK-compliant MCP server, complete organization verification on the OpenAI Platform Dashboard, submit through their dashboard with required metadata, pass their review, and get published. Our existing MCP server at `mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp` is already the right foundation -- we need to ensure it meets their specific requirements and then submit.

**Primary recommendation:** Complete OpenAI organization verification immediately, audit our MCP server against their requirements (tool annotations, CORS, error handling), prepare listing assets (icon, screenshots, descriptions), and submit.

---

## 1. Is There a Public Directory/Marketplace?

**YES.** (HIGH confidence)

The **ChatGPT App Directory** is live at [chatgpt.com/apps](https://chatgpt.com/apps). It is:

- Accessible from the ChatGPT tools menu and directly via URL
- Browsable by users (featured apps, search by name)
- The successor to the GPT Store, but for real integrations (not just prompt wrappers)
- Built entirely on MCP -- every app in the directory is an MCP server

Early apps include Spotify, Adobe Acrobat, Canva, Booking.com, MyFitnessPal, and AllTrails.

**Source:** [OpenAI - Introducing Apps in ChatGPT](https://openai.com/index/introducing-apps-in-chatgpt/), [OpenAI - Developers Can Now Submit Apps](https://openai.com/index/developers-can-now-submit-apps-to-chatgpt/)

---

## 2. How to Submit/Register an MCP Server

### Step-by-Step Submission Process

1. **Complete Organization Verification**
   - Go to the [OpenAI Platform Dashboard](https://platform.openai.com)
   - Verify individual identity (personal name) OR business identity
   - You must have the **Owner role** in your organization
   - This is a prerequisite -- cannot submit without it

2. **Ensure MCP Server Compliance**
   - Server must be publicly accessible via HTTPS
   - Must handle `POST`, `GET`, `DELETE` requests to `/mcp` path
   - Must support CORS for cross-origin requests
   - Must implement JSON-RPC 2.0 message protocol
   - Must define proper tool annotations (see Section 4)

3. **Access the Submission Dashboard**
   - Navigate to the app management section in the OpenAI Platform Dashboard
   - Input MCP server URL and OAuth credentials (if applicable)

4. **Fill Out Required Fields** (see Section 4 for full list)

5. **Submit for Review**
   - Click submit
   - Receive confirmation email with a Case ID
   - Monitor status in the Dashboard

6. **Wait for Review**
   - OpenAI performs automated scans and/or manual reviews
   - Timeline varies (no SLA published, no expedited option)
   - Approval notification comes via email

7. **Publish**
   - Approved apps transition to publication via the dashboard
   - OpenAI automatically creates a Codex plugin as well

**Source:** [Submit and Maintain Your App](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission)

---

## 3. Review / Approval Process

**YES, there is a review process.** (HIGH confidence)

OpenAI reviews every submission before it goes live. Reviews evaluate:

| Review Area | What They Check |
|-------------|----------------|
| **MCP Connectivity** | Server reachable, tools respond correctly using provided credentials |
| **Test Cases** | Your submitted test prompts produce expected responses |
| **Data Handling** | Actual behavior matches stated privacy practices |
| **Tool Annotations** | `readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `openWorldHint` are accurate |
| **Guidelines Compliance** | No prohibited content, no ads, proper naming |
| **Auth (if applicable)** | Test credentials work, 2FA is not blocking review |

### Common Rejection Reasons

- **Incorrect tool annotations** -- "a common cause of rejection" per OpenAI docs
- Connection/stability issues with MCP server
- Test case failures (expected output doesn't match)
- Undisclosed data collection
- Keyword stuffing in descriptions
- Single-word dictionary terms as app names
- Missing privacy policy
- Apps that serve advertisements

### Post-Approval Maintenance

- Apps can be removed if "inactive, unstable, or non-compliant"
- To update: create a new draft version (not a new app), add release notes, resubmit
- Only one version can be under review at a time

**Source:** [App Submission Guidelines](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/app-submission-guidelines), [Submit and Maintain Your App](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission)

---

## 4. Requirements for Listing

### Required Submission Assets

| Asset | Specification | Notes |
|-------|--------------|-------|
| **App Icon** | 64 x 64 px, under 5 KB | Small -- needs to be clean and recognizable |
| **App Name** | Max 30 characters | Avoid single-word dictionary terms. Name carries most weight in search indexation |
| **Short Description** | Brief summary | Clear, accurate, no keyword stuffing |
| **Long Description** | Detailed explanation | Explain what the app does and how |
| **Developer Name** | Organization name | Must match verified identity |
| **Privacy Policy URL** | Published policy | Must explain data categories, use purposes, recipient categories, user controls |
| **Terms & Conditions URL** | Published terms | Required |
| **Screenshots** | Must match actual functionality | Dimension requirements exist (not fully specified in docs) |
| **Verified Website** | Your domain | Must be verified |
| **MCP Server URL** | HTTPS endpoint | e.g., `https://mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp` |
| **Test Prompts & Responses** | Demo scenarios | Show expected behavior -- OpenAI tests these |
| **Localization Info** | Languages supported | Optional but recommended |
| **OAuth Credentials** | If auth required | Must include test account credentials for review |

### Tool Definition Requirements

Every tool exposed by your MCP server must have:

- **Human-readable name** -- specific, action-oriented (e.g., `get_agent_status`, `search_agents`)
- **Clear description** -- explains purpose accurately
- **Proper annotations** (CRITICAL for approval):
  - `readOnlyHint`: true for data retrieval only
  - `destructiveHint`: true for write/delete operations
  - `openWorldHint`: true for external system interactions
- **Minimal input schema** -- request only what's necessary, no "just in case" fields
- **Unique names** within the app

### Technical Requirements

- HTTPS endpoint (mandatory)
- CORS support for cross-origin requests
- JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol
- Proper error handling (no crashes, graceful degradation)
- Low latency responses
- Unique `_meta.ui.domain` per app (if using UI components)
- Content Security Policy configuration (if using UI)

**Source:** [App Submission Guidelines](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/app-submission-guidelines), [Build Your MCP Server](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/build/mcp-server), [ChatGPT App Directory Optimization](https://phiture.com/asostack/chat-gpt-app-directory/)

---

## 5. Submission API/Form

**Dashboard-based submission** (no public API). (HIGH confidence)

- Submissions go through the **OpenAI Platform Dashboard** (platform.openai.com)
- There is a dedicated app management section
- Fill in the form, upload assets, submit
- Track review status in the same dashboard
- Email notifications for status changes

There is **no public API** for programmatic submission. It is a manual dashboard process.

**Note:** "Self-serve plugin publishing is coming soon" according to OpenAI docs, suggesting the process may become more automated in the future.

**Source:** [Submit and Maintain Your App](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission)

---

## 6. Featured/Curated Connectors

**YES, there is a featured/curated tier.** (HIGH confidence)

OpenAI distinguishes between basic publication and enhanced distribution:

| Tier | What It Means |
|------|--------------|
| **Published** | App appears in directory, searchable by name |
| **Featured/Enhanced Distribution** | Directory placement, proactive suggestions by ChatGPT |

### How to Get Featured

Apps demonstrating **"strong real-world utility and high user satisfaction"** may receive:
- Prominent directory placement
- Proactive recommendations (ChatGPT suggests the app during relevant conversations)

This is separate from basic publication approval -- it is earned through usage and quality, not applied for directly.

### Discovery Mechanics (Current State)

- **Name-based search** is the primary discovery method
- Name carries the **most weight** in search indexation
- Long-tail keyword matching is limited currently
- Individual keywords perform better than combined phrases
- No category browsing system documented yet (browsing appears to be search + featured)

**Source:** [App Submission Guidelines](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/app-submission-guidelines), [ChatGPT App Directory Optimization](https://phiture.com/asostack/chat-gpt-app-directory/)

---

## 7. The ChatGPT "Apps" Ecosystem (Full Picture)

### Architecture

```
ChatGPT App = MCP Server + (Optional) Web UI Component
                |
                v
         Apps SDK (OpenAI's framework)
                |
                v
         App Directory (the marketplace)
                |
                v
         Also creates Codex Plugin automatically
```

### Key Terminology

| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **App** | The new name (replaced "Connector") -- an MCP server integrated with ChatGPT |
| **Connector** | Legacy term, being phased out in favor of "App" |
| **Apps SDK** | OpenAI's framework for building ChatGPT apps (wraps MCP) |
| **App Directory** | The marketplace at chatgpt.com/apps |
| **Developer Mode** | Testing mode in ChatGPT settings for unreviewed apps |
| **Codex Plugin** | Auto-created when app is published (for OpenAI's Codex product) |

### Optional UI Component

Apps can optionally include a web UI component that renders inside ChatGPT:
- Built as HTML/CSS/JS (or React, Vue, etc.)
- Rendered in an iframe
- Communicates via JSON-RPC over postMessage
- MIME type: `text/html;profile=mcp-app`
- Not required -- tools-only apps work fine

### Commerce Restrictions

If planning any transactional features:
- **Physical goods only** currently (no digital products, subscriptions, tokens, credits)
- External checkout required (redirect to your domain)
- No embedded third-party checkout
- No advertisements allowed

### Developer Mode (For Testing Before Submission)

Before submitting, you can test your MCP server in ChatGPT:
1. Settings > Apps & Connectors > Advanced settings > Enable Developer Mode
2. Settings > Connectors > Create
3. Enter HTTPS URL, name, description
4. Test in new chats by adding the connector from the + menu

---

## 8. Action Plan for OpenBotCity MCP Server

### Immediate Steps

1. **Verify Organization on OpenAI Platform**
   - Go to platform.openai.com
   - Complete business identity verification
   - Ensure you have Owner role

2. **Audit MCP Server Compliance**
   - Verify CORS headers on `mcp.openbotcity.com/mcp`
   - Ensure JSON-RPC 2.0 compliance
   - Add tool annotations (`readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `openWorldHint`) to ALL tools
   - Test error handling (graceful failures, no crashes)
   - Verify low-latency responses

3. **Prepare Listing Assets**
   - Design 64x64 px icon (under 5 KB)
   - Write app name (max 30 chars) -- e.g., "OpenBotCity" or "OpenBotCity AI City"
   - Write short + long descriptions (clear, no keyword stuffing)
   - Prepare screenshots showing actual tool functionality
   - Publish privacy policy and terms at openbotcity.com URLs
   - Verify openbotcity.com domain

4. **Write Test Prompts**
   - Create 3-5 test prompts that demonstrate core functionality
   - Document expected responses for each
   - These will be tested during review

5. **Test in Developer Mode**
   - Enable Developer Mode in ChatGPT
   - Add as connector, test thoroughly
   - Fix any issues found

6. **Submit**
   - Fill out all fields in the OpenAI Platform Dashboard
   - Submit and track via Case ID

### Naming Strategy

Since name carries the most weight in search:
- "OpenBotCity" is clean and unique (good)
- Consider if adding a descriptor helps: "OpenBotCity - AI Agent City"
- Avoid generic terms that might get rejected

---

## Prohibited Content Checklist

Verify our MCP server does NOT involve:

- [x] No adult content
- [x] No gambling
- [x] No illegal goods
- [x] No prescription medications
- [x] No surveillance tools
- [x] No advertisements served through the app
- [x] No digital product sales (currently restricted)

---

## Open Questions

1. **Screenshot dimensions** -- exact pixel requirements for screenshots not fully documented. Prepare multiple sizes (1280x800, 1920x1080) to be safe.

2. **Review timeline** -- OpenAI explicitly says "timelines may vary" with no SLA. Early reports suggest days to weeks. Being in beta means this could fluctuate.

3. **Category system** -- no formal category taxonomy documented yet. The directory appears to rely on search + featured placement rather than browsable categories.

4. **Proactive suggestion criteria** -- what exactly triggers ChatGPT to proactively suggest your app is not documented beyond "strong real-world utility and high user satisfaction."

5. **Codex Plugin auto-creation** -- when your app is published, OpenAI also creates a Codex plugin. Unclear if this requires any additional configuration from us.

---

## Sources

### Primary (HIGH confidence)
- [OpenAI Apps SDK Quickstart](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/quickstart)
- [Build Your MCP Server](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/build/mcp-server)
- [Connect from ChatGPT](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/connect-chatgpt)
- [Submit and Maintain Your App](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission)
- [App Submission Guidelines](https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/app-submission-guidelines)
- [OpenAI - Developers Can Now Submit Apps](https://openai.com/index/developers-can-now-submit-apps-to-chatgpt/)
- [OpenAI - Introducing Apps in ChatGPT](https://openai.com/index/introducing-apps-in-chatgpt/)

### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
- [ChatGPT App Directory Optimization (Phiture)](https://phiture.com/asostack/chat-gpt-app-directory/)
- [VentureBeat - OpenAI App Submissions](https://venturebeat.com/technology/openai-now-accepting-chatgpt-app-submissions-from-third-party-devs-launches)
- [OpenAI Community Forum - App Store Submissions](https://community.openai.com/t/chatgpt-app-store-is-open-for-submissions/1369611)

### Also Relevant
- [MCP Registry](https://modelcontextprotocol.info/tools/registry/) -- separate from ChatGPT, this is the community MCP registry (Anthropic, GitHub, Microsoft backed). Consider listing there too.
- [MCP Market](https://mcpmarket.com/) -- third-party MCP server discovery platform
